---
title: "Andrés Martínez Glez"
subtitle: "Economist · AIReF · Madrid"
image: profile.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 12em
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/AndresMtnezGlez
    - icon: file-earmark-text
      text: RPubs
      href: https://rpubs.com/andresmtnezglez
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/YOUR-LINKEDIN
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:YOUR@EMAIL
---

I am an economist at the **Autoridad Independiente de Responsabilidad Fiscal (AIReF)**, Spain's independent fiscal institution, based in Madrid.

My work sits at the intersection of applied fiscal policy, econometrics, and institutional analysis. I work on EU and Spanish fiscal governance — the net expenditure path, the control account, and the reformed economic governance framework — as well as macroeconomic forecast endorsements, long-term growth modelling, and the labour-market effects of regulatory reform.

I currently lead the project on external reviews of independent fiscal institutions within the Economic Governance Reform Working Group of the Network of EU Independent Fiscal Institutions (EUNIFI).

Most of my applied work is in **R**: time-series and VAR estimation, forecasting, and reproducible reporting.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Views expressed on this site are my own and do not represent those of AIReF.
:::

## Research interests

- EU fiscal governance and the reformed fiscal rules
- Macroeconometrics: VAR and structural VAR models, time-series forecasting
- Long-term growth and potential output modelling
- Labour market and social security microdata analysis
